_deredact_applied.fix: Replaced `func REDACTED(t *testing.T)` with `func TestExplainCmd_ShowsHelpForPositionalArgWithoutFlag(t *testing.T)` in patch field. The literal token REDACTED prevented `go test` from running the canonical-added test (test functions must start with `Test`), capping the canonical patch reward at 0.0 instead of 1.0. Upstream commit 4993e6e3 confirms the real function name via `gh api repos/entireio/cli/commits/4993e6e3`. install_config.json FAIL_TO_PASS already lists this name. No other REDACTED tokens were present in the patch field. Fidelity upgraded directional -> exact: only remaining warning is a formatter-anchor mismatch on explain.go (op[4]), independent of the test name; patch body content now matches upstream exactly.

_fix_applied.fix: Removed TestExplainSession_NotFound from install_config.json FAIL_TO_PASS. The round-2 audit had it inverted: TestExplainSession_NotFound already exists at base commit b386f5fa (verified via gh api repos/entireio/cli/contents/cmd/entire/cli/explain_test.go?ref=b386f5fa — 45 test functions present including TestExplainSession_NotFound), so it is pass-to-pass, not fail-to-pass. The local canonical patch shows the new test as `func REDACTED(t *testing.T)` because test names are scrubbed in this benchmark's stored patches (REDACTED appears throughout original_session.json) — but upstream commit 4993e6e3 confirms the actual added test is named `TestExplainCmd_ShowsHelpForPositionalArgWithoutFlag` (verified via gh api repos/entireio/cli/commits/4993e6e3). So the genuinely fabricated F2P entry was TestExplainSession_NotFound (existed at baseline), not TestExplainCmd_ShowsHelpForPositionalArgWithoutFlag (which is the real new test, just locally redacted). Canonical patch will now score 1/1 = 1.0.
